headline: Emmanuel Grégoire takes office as the new Paris mayor
subhead: On Sunday, the Socialist was officially elected by his peers on the Council of Paris, in which the political balance of power remains largely unchanged after the municipal elections. The left will govern for a fifth consecutive term.

headline: UN force in Lebanon says soldier killed in country's south
In wartime Tehran, rare photos capture daily life under bombardment
Flavel, a victim of police violence, tells his story: 'If there hadn't been videos, no one would ever have believed me'
subhead: On March 16, the former musician went out to order food in Noisiel, in the eastern Paris suburbs. Police violently arrested him, as seen in a video widely shared online. He described to Le Monde the events that led him to file a complaint with the internal affairs agency.

headline: Migrant Channel crossings: France-UK negotiations over border funding get bogged down
subhead: France and the UK are struggling to reach an agreement on a new British funding cycle as London, under pressure from British public opinion, has demanded greater results from Paris in preventing migrants from crossing the English Channel.

headline: Is it ever a good idea to... wear leather pants?
subhead: In fashion, as in life, it is important to know the lines you should not cross. Or do so deliberately.

headline: Adrien Bilal, winner of the 2026 Best Young Economist Award: 'Climate change will cost 50% of GDP by 2100'
subhead: The winner of the 27th edition of the prize, created in 2000 by Le Monde and Le Cercle des Economistes, has developed a model that better captures the global impacts of climate disruption.

Christian Jambet, Islamic scholar: 'The true leaders of Iran are now soldiers who want to spread the Islamic Revolution beyond Iran's border'
subhead: The philosopher and member of the Académie Française, an expert on Shiite spiritualities, shares his thoughts on the ongoing war and the 'degeneration' of the Iranian regime.

headline: Anne Hidalgo's unwavering tenacity after 12 years as Paris mayor
subhead: After being elected to the Council of Paris in 2001, Hidalgo was immediately appointed as then-mayor Bertrand Delanoë's first deputy, before becoming mayor herself in 2014. Over her two terms in office, her determination, despite strong political opposition and legal obstacles, set her apart.

headline: Volodymyr Zelensky's exclusive interview with Le Monde on the war in Ukraine
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, the Ukrainian president shares his perspective on a possible end to the conflict and urges the US and all allied nations to maintain their support for Ukraine.
